growth_records.cite: Tan KH et al. Porphyromonas gingivalis and Treponema denticola Exhibit Metabolic Symbioses. PLoS Pathogens (2014). DOI:10.1371/journal.ppat.1003955. [PMC3946380]
growth_records.snippet: The three T. denticola mono-cultures also reached steady state approximately 8 days after inoculation with an average A650 nm of 0.23±0.02 equating to 7.7±0.7×10^8 cells mL−1 ... dilution rate of 0.044 h−1 and mean generation time of 15.8 h
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: at steady state mu = dilution rate D = 0.044 h-1 (reported; flow 39 mL/h / 900 mL = 0.0433/h, paper rounds to 0.044). Doubling/mean generation time = ln2/0.044 = 15.75 h (paper states 15.8 h). Steady-state cell density 7.7±0.7×10^8 cells/mL at A650=0.23±0.02. No substrate uptake or product secretion rates (mmol/gDW/h) reported for the chemostat; only growth reported. pH of OBGM not stated. OBGM composition cited from refs, not given.

up.units: mM total consumed (completely consumed between 24 h and 144 h, i.e. over ~120 h)

sec.units: mM produced (stated underestimate due to CO2 equilibration)

mfa.note: 3.67 mM acetate / 5.00 mM glycine consumed; acetate major uniformly-labeled end-product; ~73% of glycine label recovered in acetate (reduced via glycine reductase system)
1.0 mM lactate / 5.00 mM glycine; proposed via serine->pyruvate->lactate
0.17 mM / 5.00 mM; reported as underestimate (loss to CO2 atmosphere)

growth_records.cite: Tan KH et al. Porphyromonas gingivalis and Treponema denticola Exhibit Metabolic Symbioses. PLoS Pathogens (2014). DOI:10.1371/journal.ppat.1003955. [PMC3946380]
growth_records.snippet: [U-13C]glycine was completely consumed by 144 h, with production of 13C-acetate (3.67 mM) and 13C-lactate (1 mM) ... Catabolism of 13C-glycine was also associated with the production of H13CO3 ... yields of H13CO3 (0.17 mM) are likely to be
growth_records.notes: PRIORITY experimental record (13C-labeling fermentation). Measured: 5.00 mM [U-13C]glycine added to 24 h culture, completely consumed by 144 h; products acetate 3.67 mM, lactate 1.0 mM, bicarbonate 0.17 mM (underestimate), trace alanine. Product yields computed from explicit mM: acetate/glycine = 3.67/5.00 = 0.734 mol/mol; lactate/glycine = 1.0/5.00 = 0.20 mol/mol; HCO3/glycine = 0.17/5.00 = 0.034 mol/mol. Derived volumetric average rates over the 120 h consumption window (144-24 h): glycine uptake = 5.00/120 = 0.042 mmol/L/h; acetate = 3.67/120 = 0.031 mmol/L/h; lactate = 1.0/120 = 0.0083 mmol/L/h. Cannot convert to mmol/gDW/h: no biomass/gDW reported for this batch. In a related experiment starting OBGM glycine (1.46 mM) was rapidly depleted over 72 h coinciding with entry to stationary phase; 10 mM glycine supplementation raised final cell density ~1.75-fold. Carbon products acetate+lactate; ATP via substrate-level phosphorylation (glycine reductase system).
